title: How to Safely Pack and Move Large Traditional Clay Mitads (Injera Grills) Without Cracking
excerpt: Moving mitads and injera grills: clay protection, shock padding, vertical transport, traditional kitchen items, Ethiopian home moves, packing guide.
content:
How to safely pack and move large traditional clay mitads (injera grills) without cracking

A large clay mitad is kitchen hearth—not cookware you toss in a carton. Thermal clay, glazed or raw, cracks from point loads, corner impacts, and temperature shock when wrapped wet or stacked under wardrobes. Moving mitads demands shock engineering scaled for tradition: vertical orientation, double boxing, no stack weight, and truck placement that respects what Sunday injera depends on.

This guide covers packing and transport for mitads and large injera grills in Ethiopian household moves.

Assess mitad type and condition

  • Inspect hairline cracks before move—photo for insurance; inform crew.
  • Note glazed versus raw clay—glaze chips propagate cracks.
  • Measure diameter and rim height—custom box sizing.
  • Identify irreplaceable heirloom versus replaceable daily mitad—carry policy differs.

Pre-move preparation

Clean and fully dry—moisture in wrap grows mold and weakens clay in vibration. Remove from built-in platforms if modular; bag mounting hardware labeled. Never move hot mitad—cool 24 hours minimum.

Packing method

Primary wrap

Bubble or foam sheet on cooking face and rim; cardboard disc on flat surfaces distributing pressure.

Double box or crate

Inner box sized to mitad with two-inch shock fill; outer box with additional padding—mitad must not shift when carton shaken.

Orientation

Vertical or flat with even support—never edge-loaded on rim only. Mark THIS SIDE UP on two faces.

Truck placement

Top load, strapped vertical stack; no furniture on mitad carton. Away from tailgate heat and tool boxes. Last on, first off—deliver to kitchen counter, not curb.

Unpacking and first use

Open over soft surface; inspect rim under light. Wait 24 hours before first fire if long cold transport—gradual heat reduces thermal shock.

Vendor pack with movers

Brief Active Movers crew—mitad cartons never under stack; fragile top shelf only. Declare on inventory for claims.

Built-in mitad platforms

Photograph gas or electrical disconnect before removal—reinstall by qualified tech at destination.

Conclusion

Move mitads like ceremonial ceramics: dry, double-boxed, top-loaded, gently unwrapped—and injera Sundays survive the new kitchen intact.
author: Active Movers Team
